summary: Draft closed PR for melee player bonus code. It replaced the placeholder for fn_80039618 in src/melee/pl/plbonus.c with a large partial C implementation, adjusted digit arithmetic in fn_8003CC84, wrapped fn_80038700 in a dont_inline pragma block, and typed several fields in pl_804D6470_t. Bot report claimed 3 new matches and fn_80039618 improvement to 99.67%, but the PR was closed unmerged and has no human review comments.

key_files.role: Main decomp change: implemented fn_80039618, added dont_inline around fn_80038700, changed arithmetic in fn_8003CC84, and used PAD_STACK in the new function.
Header/type recovery: replaced byte padding in pl_804D6470_t with typed float/u32/int fields used by plbonus.c.

what_changed.text: Replaced /// #fn_80039618 with a large C body that reads plActionStats, stale-move table state, global pl_804D6470 thresholds, and game/player helper functions, then sets many bonus flags through setFlag.
Added #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around fn_80038700.
Changed fn_8003CC84 digit extraction from chained modulo expressions to subtracting the hundreds and tens components before division/subtraction.
Typed selected pl_804D6470_t offsets, including x0, x8, xC, x10, x20, x50, x54, xF0, and x160, replacing byte padding at those offsets.
Bot report for GALE01 claimed matched code +1640 bytes, matched data +456 bytes, 3 new matches including plbonus .data, fn_8003CC84, .sdata2, and fn_80039618 improvement from 0.00% to 99.67%.

smart_moves.text: Recovered pl_804D6470_t field types at offsets directly used by the new plbonus logic instead of continuing to use opaque byte arrays for those accesses.
Kept many repeated scan patterns as explicit for-loops over attack/count arrays rather than hand-expanding repeated cases.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:fn_80039618 loops over hit_counts, x358_counts, player indices, and move ranges

smart_moves.text: Used a grouped switch for selected move IDs while accumulating sub_sum, matching the canonical-control-flow preference for switch forms when adjacent cases share behavior.

decomp_lessons.text: When a decomp body depends on threshold/config values from an existing struct, typing only the proven offsets can make the implementation more readable and reduce raw offset access. In this PR the typed pl_804D6470_t offsets are all used by fn_80039618.

Arithmetic rewrites that are mathematically equivalent to modulo chains can affect matching; fn_8003CC84 changed digit extraction from % forms to subtraction forms, and the bot report listed fn_8003CC84 as a new 100% match.

Large near-match functions may still contain matching tactics that require extra scrutiny; this PR used PAD_STACK(16) and a dont_inline pragma while the bot report showed fn_80039618 at 99.67%, not a complete function match.

naming_conventions.text: The PR preserved address-style names for unrecovered functions and fields, such as fn_80039618, fn_80038700, pl_80038628, pl_804D6470_t, xD04, xD0C, xDD1, and x160, while using local role names like table, stats, gobj, hit_counts, atk_counts, sum_attacks, and max_ratio.
No PR evidence explains semantic meanings for many flag IDs or x-offset fields, so the address/offset-style names should not be treated as resolved semantics.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl:empty PR body and no human review comments

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used #pragma dont_inline around fn_80038700, scoped with #pragma push and #pragma pop.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No human explanation or adjacent-function regression evidence is present in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Inserted PAD_STACK(16) at the end of fn_80039618.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Bot report shows fn_80039618 improved to 99.67%, but not a full match; the slice does not show why this padding is required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Changed digit arithmetic in fn_8003CC84 to avoid repeated modulo operations.
Used gotos as skip labels for early exit from scan blocks inside fn_80039618.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No evidence in the slice proves whether these gotos were necessary for matching or could be expressed as structured control flow.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 claimed matched code 67.81% (+0.04%, +1640 bytes), matched data 39.68% (+0.04%, +456 bytes), 3 new matches, and fn_80039618 improvement from 0.00% to 99.67%.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl contains PR body and one bot issue comment only

notes: Strong diff evidence for exactly which files and code changed.
Bot report provides matching-progress claims, including fn_8003CC84 reaching 100% and fn_80039618 reaching 99.67%.
No PR body, human review comments, or author explanation are present.
PR is closed with merged_at null and marked draft in raw PR JSON; do not treat the changes as accepted upstream.
No local build log, objdiff details, adjacent-function regression check, or explanation for pragma/PAD_STACK tactics is included.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed config fields can support readable decomp bodies when offsets are directly used
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2523, fn_80039618 used many pl_804D6470 threshold fields, and the PR changed pl_804D6470_t padding at those offsets into typed float/u32/int fields. This is a graph-safe example of type recovery tied to a decomp body, not proof that all field names or semantics are known.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Modulo-to-subtraction arithmetic rewrite affected fn_8003CC84 matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR rewrote digit extraction in fn_8003CC84 from temp_ret % 100 forms to subtraction from the hundreds/tens components. The bot report then listed fn_8003CC84 as improving from 97.94% to 100.00%.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit justification for dont_inline and PAD_STACK tactics in near-match PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2523 used a scoped #pragma dont_inline on fn_80038700 and PAD_STACK(16) in fn_80039618, while the bot report showed fn_80039618 only improved to 99.67%. Consider reinforcing that such tactics should include narrow objdiff/build evidence and a note explaining why cleaner source forms did not suffice.
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards already warn that pragmas and PAD_STACK need evidence; this PR is a concrete review example where the slice lacks that explanation.

source_update_candidates.title: plbonus.c owns player bonus flag calculation work around fn_80039618
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/pl/plbonus.c contains player bonus logic involving plActionStats, stale move tables, pl_804D6470 thresholds, and setFlag calls. PR 2523 attempted to decompile fn_80039618 in that file.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future path scoping, but should be checked against current source before promotion because this PR was closed unmerged.

rejection_notes: Do not promote fn_80039618 as matched; bot report says 99.67%, not 100%.
Do not infer merge or acceptance; metadata shows CLOSED and merged_at null.
Do not infer human reviewer intent; no reviews or human review comments are present.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(16), dont_inline, gotos, or the specific pl_804D6470_t field typings as project-wide style from this PR alone.
Do not assign semantic names to x-offset fields or flag IDs based only on this diff.
